note:
2013年，马萨诸塞州选民通过了一项法律，要求汽车制造商向第三方维修店销售诊断软件。这是美国第一部汽车维修权法律，也是对汽车制造商封锁除各自特许维修网络以外的所有人获取汽车诊断数据的不断升级的趋势的回应。今年，活动家们回到投票箱，将州法律扩大到现在包括任何无线(或远程信息处理)数据。
马萨诸塞州现有维修权法律的一部分要求，从2018年车型年起，每辆车都有一个非专有的车辆接口设备(总是一个OBDII端口)，车主和独立车库可以通过它获取诊断信息。
但汽车业正在走向无线。与欧盟不同的是，联邦政府并没有要求每辆新车都要嵌入调制解调器，但如果一辆新车或卡车没有某种形式的车载连接，并且在遇险时不能与家进行通信，那么买一辆新车或卡车仍然变得越来越困难。
这就是马萨诸塞州问题1的用武之地。如果获得通过，它将扩大现有立法的范围，使其也包括远程信息处理，它将远程信息处理定义为：
机动车辆中的任何系统，它收集车辆运行产生的信息，并利用无线通信将这些信息(在本章中称为“远程信息处理系统数据”)传输到存储这些信息的远程接收点。
如果通过，由于要求实施新的用户身份验证系统(或完全放弃任何使用身份验证)，每个OEM&#39；连接的CAR平台都需要进行重大更改：
尽管有前款规定，机动车车主和独立维修机构对车载诊断系统的访问应标准化，直接或间接不需要制造商的任何授权，除非访问车辆网络及其车载诊断系统的授权系统在英联邦销售的所有品牌和车型上都是标准化的，并且由与制造商无关的实体管理。
投票措施还要求，从2022年车型年开始，所有装有远程信息处理系统的车辆都必须使用标准化的、开放接入的、可互操作的远程信息处理系统，该系统还必须能够将其所有数据直接发送给最终用户(而不是从车辆到云再到最终用户)。
最后，车辆架构将需要支持双向通信，包括在需要时为维护、诊断和维修目的向车内部件发送命令的能力。
如果你认为这听起来像是一种可怕的数字安全方法，实施时间极短(鉴于我们现在是在2021年)，那么你并不孤单。美国国家公路交通安全管理局(National Highway Traffic Safety Administration)告诉马萨诸塞州消费者保护和专业执照联合委员会(Joint Committee On Consumer Protection And Professional Licsure)，它认为这项投票措施含糊不清，它没有反映任何解决网络安全风险的既定最佳做法或其他措施。
note_en:
In 2013, voters in Massachusetts passed a law that requires car manufacturers to sell diagnostic software to third-party repair shops. It was the first automotive "right to repair" law in the country and was a response to the escalating trend of automakers blocking access to vehicle diagnostic data to everyone other than their respective franchised repair networks. This year, campaigners are returning to the ballot box to expand the state law to now include any wireless (or telematic) data.
 Part of Massachusetts' existing right to repair law requires, from model year 2018 onward, that every vehicle has "a non-proprietary vehicle interface device"—invariably an OBDII port—by which owners and independent garages can access diagnostic information.
 But the auto industry is going wireless.  Unlike in the European Union, there's no federal requirement for an embedded modem in every new vehicle, but it's still becoming hard to buy a new car or truck that doesn't have some form of onboard connectivity and  the ability to communicate with home when it's in distress.
 That's where  Massachusetts Question 1 comes in. If passed, it would extend the existing legislation such that it would also cover telematics, which it defines as:
 any system in a motor vehicle that collects information generated by the operation of the vehicle and transmits such information, in this chapter referred to as “telematics system data,” utilizing wireless communications to a remote receiving point where it is stored.
 If passed, every OEM's connected car platform would need significant changes due to the requirement to implement a new system for user authentication (or drop any use authentication at all):
 Notwithstanding anything in the preceding paragraph, motor vehicle owners’ and independent repair facilities’ access to vehicle on-board diagnostic systems shall be standardized and not require any authorization by the manufacturer, directly or indirectly, unless the authorization system for access to vehicle networks and their on-board diagnostic systems is standardized across all makes and models sold in the Commonwealth and is administered by an entity unaffiliated with a manufacturer.
 The ballot measure also requires that from model year 2022, all vehicles with telematics systems must use standardized, open-access, interoperable telematics systems, which must also be able to send all of their data directly to an end user (as opposed to going from the vehicle to the cloud to the end user).
 Finally, the vehicle architecture will need to support two-way communications, including "the ability to send commands to in-vehicle components if needed for purposes of maintenance, diagnostics and repair."
 If you think that sounds like a scary approach to digital security with an extremely short timeline for implementation (given that we are now in MY2021), you are not alone. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration told the Massachusetts Joint Committee on Consumer Protection and Professional Licensure that it considered the ballot measure  to be vague and that it did not "reflect any established best-practices or other measures to address cybersecurity risks."
title: 马萨诸塞州是否应该将维修权法律扩展到联网汽车？
title_en: Should Massachusetts extend its right-to-repair law to connected cars?

note:
在考虑下一步该做什么的时候，他快速浏览了一下InsightFinder，然后将注意力转向了每个初创公司创始人都会面临的一个问题：为你的财务未来建立模型。“Finmark是初创企业的财务模型。我们希望帮助创始人了解他们的秀台，他们的现金流，他们的招聘计划，并能够以一种简单的方式做到这一点，“Essay告诉TechCrunch。
这是他在Distil联合创始人时亲眼目睹的问题。像大多数初创公司一样，这些预测在Excel中有点像垃圾。他希望在你买得起更复杂的财务跟踪软件之前，让你更精确、更容易地了解你公司的财务状况。
他说：“最大的痛点之一总是理解我们的预测相对于我们作为一家公司实际在做什么。很多时候，我们的整个业务都依赖于Excel，很多次，我们认为我们要做的事情的预测都是错误的，“他说。
他说，这已经够难的了，即使你聘请了第一位首席财务官，并对你的预测应用了专业的严谨态度。在他看来，问题是你总是回头看，总是在追赶。更重要的是，因为它是在Excel中手动完成的，他说这会带来很大的出错空间。
他承认这并不完全是一个新想法。像Anaplan和Adaptive Insight这样的公司已经能够将这样的建模转移到Excel之外，但是到目前为止，他说这些工具是为大型企业设计的，他想要想出一个任何人都能拿到的工具，无论他们的大小。
如果你认为这是一个太有限的市场，埃萨德不同意。他看到了需求，他认为他可以将早期初创公司转变为付费客户，这些客户最终会支付大笔资金，拥有这样的工具，帮助他们以专业的方式管理所有财务。建立他的客户基础的一种方式可能是与早期风险资本家合作，他们的投资组合公司可以从这样的服务中受益，这是他打算追求的一条途径。
那么，为什么会有经验丰富的企业家加入Y Combinator呢？Essay坦率地说，他认为这个项目是营销产品的一个很好的方式。YC公司是他的主要目标受众。他说：“即使作为一名留着一些白发的重复创始人，我认为仅接入该网络一项就值得YC的股权。”
但他说，除了实践方面，他还有很多东西要学。他说：“即使我学到了所有的经验教训，你也不是什么都懂，而且他们看到的公司比我有机会经营的公司多得多，所以我在参加这个项目时仍然找到了智慧的金子。”
虽然Essay旗下有一家公司，专门为STEM的女性提供数十万美元的奖学金，但他承认，很难建立一个多元化的组织，这是他仍在努力的事情。他与两名前Distil工程师共同创立了这家公司，他表示，随着他增加早期员工，人们自然而然地倾向于回到之前在Distil共事的人，但他认识到，他不一定会以这种方式发展一个多元化的员工群体。
“我还没有解决这个问题的答案。[…]。我们一直在从前蒸馏器公司招聘，但一旦我们把目光投向外部，我认为以一种你可以看起来的方式来设置东西真的很重要[…]。以公正的眼光看待简历，“他说。
目前有15名员工在发工资，他只是想把公司建设好。他暗示，他正在努力获得资金，但目前还没有任何确定的说法。
note_en:
As he pondered what to do next, he took a quick turn at InsightFinder before turning his attention to a problem every startup founder faces: modeling what your financial future could look like. “Finmark is financial modeling for startups. We want to help founders understand their runway, their cash flow, their hiring plans and be able to do it in an easy way,” Essaid told TechCrunch.
 It’s a problem he saw first-hand when he was co-founder at Distil. Like most startups, these projections were kind of a crapshoot in Excel. He wants to make it more precise and easy to get the big picture of your company’s finances before you can afford more sophisticated financial tracking software
  “One of the biggest pain points was always understanding where our projections were relative to what we were actually doing as a company. So many times we were running our entire business off of Excel, and so many times the forecasts of what we thought we were going to do were wrong,” he said.
 He says it’s tough enough, even after you hire your first CFO and have professional rigor applied to your projections. As he sees it, the problem is you’re always looking back and always playing catchup. What’s more, because it’s done manually in Excel, he says that it introduces a lot of room for error.
  He admits this isn’t exactly a new idea. Companies like Anaplan and Adaptive Insights have been able to move modeling like this beyond Excel, but up until now he says that these tools have been designed for large enterprises, and he wanted to come up with a tool within reach of anyone, regardless of their size.
 If you think it’s too limited a market, Essaid doesn’t agree. He sees a need and he thinks he can turn early stage startups into paying customers, who eventually will pay significant money to have a tool like this to help them manage all of their finances in a professional manner. One way to build his customer base could be to partner with early stage venture capitalists, whose portfolio companies could benefit from a service like this, an avenue he intends to pursue.
 So why does an experienced entrepreneur join  Y Combinator?   Essaid candidly says that he saw the program as a good way to market the product. YC companies are his prime target audience. “Even as a repeat founder with some gray hair, I thought access to the network alone was worth the equity of YC,” he said.
 But beyond the practical aspects, he says he still has plenty to learn. “Even with all of the lessons that I have learned, you don’t know everything, and they see a lot more companies than the ones that I’ve had a chance to operate, so I still find nuggets of wisdom in going through the program,” he said.
 While Essaid has a company under his belt, which dedicated hundreds of thousands of dollars to scholarships for women in STEM, he admits that it’s hard to build a diverse organization and it’s something he’s still working on. He co-founded the company with two ex-Distil engineers, and he says there is a natural inclination to go back to the people he worked with before at Distil as he adds early employees, but he recognizes that he will not necessarily grow a diverse group of employees that way.
 “I don’t have an answer to solving it yet. […] We’ve been hiring from ex-Distilers but once we look outside of that, I think it’s really important to set up things in a way where you can look […] at resumes with an unbiased lens,” he said.
 For now with 15 employees on the payroll, he’s just trying to build the company. He hinted that he is working on obtaining funding, but didn’t have anything definitive to say just yet.
title: Finmark希望将复杂的金融建模放在初创公司的触手可及之处
title_en: Finmark wants to put sophisticated financial modeling within reach of startups

note:
不是很好，真的。我敢打赌，你接受新演出的经历一定有些相似。在初创公司中，提供的工作都有异国情调的薪酬，在他们所有的409a和归属时期的荣耀中都充满了初创公司的股票期权。有些人可能并不真正理解所提供的是什么。比如，当绩效工资和其他甜头叠加在基本工资之上时，他们全套薪酬的真正价值是什么。在等式中有远程学习，这就更令人困惑了。
这就是初创公司Welcome想要修复的市场空间，该公司宣布了140万美元的融资。
该公司告诉TechCrunch，这是一个“第一报价管理和成交平台”。它的服务有助于向应聘者提供全面薪酬的清晰画面，帮助他们接受或拒绝他们完全理解的提议。
以下是应聘者在雇主和雇员之间分歧方面的截图：
如果“提供管理和关闭”听起来像是一个可以瞄准的小众市场，那么它既是也不是。
这是因为，如果Welcome永远保持目前的市场地位，它的产品目标将比大多数初创公司小。但该公司计划随着时间的推移扩大其产品系列。例如，它的联合创始人尼克·加夫隆斯基(Nick Gavronsky)和里克·佩雷拉(Rick Pereira)解释说，Welcome希望在未来提供实时的工资数据，基于将通过其服务流动的信息。
想要对北卡罗来纳州的一名工程师充满信心地结业吗？欢迎应该能够告诉你，稍后，如果你想确保候选人会接受，薪酬包应该是什么样子的。
Gavronsky和Pereira分别在产品和人员工作方面有经验，这使得他们在Welcome的结合非常合适。该公司的团队目前只有四个人，尽管这家初创公司预计今年的规模将翻一番。它在1月份筹集的资金，但现在只在谈论，正在使招聘成为可能。
现在，140万美元这个数字已经相当过时了。通常情况下，我会跳过与过去相去甚远的一轮，但Welcome引起了我的注意，因为我最近写了一篇关于另一家人力资源技术提供商苍井空(Sora)的文章，而Welcome的交易感觉就像是一个例证：这就是种子轮的宣布方式，在事实发生很久之后，这使得报道种子期趋势变得非常困难。有些事情要牢记在心。
欢迎用它的产品造就了一棵迷人的树，这不仅是因为今天的报价/报价接受过程是垃圾-让我们通过电子邮件发送一些PDF，并在部门之间递送一名候选人！-还因为它看到了潜在客户的强劲早期需求。它的服务目前是一个私人Alpha，订阅量有点超额，尽管该公司还没有对其服务收费。(欢迎使用SaaS实演，按公司规模定价，这似乎很合理)。
除此之外，Welcome的令人兴奋之处在于，如果它在进行测试版或发布时能够获得大量客户，那么该公司将处于一个可以向多个方向扩张的位置。例如，考虑到它已经知道一位新的候选人和他们的新雇主，它可以扩展其功能集，以帮助进行入职前或入职本身。当然，这家初创公司想要更多地谈论它今天正在建设的东西，但展望未来也是一件有趣的事情。
欢迎到此为止，当他们正式发布时，我们会再次谈论他们，或者分享一些整洁的增长指标。在此之前，祝你顺利进入阿尔法。
note_en:
Not great, really. I bet that your experience accepting new gigs has been somewhat similar. In startups, jobs are offered with exotic types of pay, chock full of startup stock options in all their 409a and vesting-period glory. Some folks might not really understand what is being offered. Like what the value of their full comp package really is, when performance pay and other sweeteners are stacked on top of base rates. With remote learning in the equation, it’s even more confusing.
 This is the market space that  Welcome, a startup that is announcing a $1.4 million fundraise, wants to fix.
 The company told TechCrunch it is a “first offer management and closing platform.” Its service helps provide a clear picture of total comp to candidates, helping them accept or deny an offer that they can fully understand.
 Here’s a screengrab, from the candidate’s side of the employer-employee divide:
  If “offer management and closing” sounds like a small niche to target, it both is and is not.
 It is, in that if Welcome stayed in its current market-position forever it would have a smaller product target than most startups. But the company has plans to expand its product-set over time. For example, its co-founders  Nick Gavronsky and  Rick Pereira explained that Welcome wants to offer real-time salary data in the future, based on the information that will flow through its service.
 Want to close a engineer in North Carolina with a high level of confidence in the offer? Welcome should be able to tell you, later on, what a comp package should look like if you want make sure the candidate will accept.
 Gavronsky and Pereira have experience in product and people work, respectively, making their union at Welcome a good fit. The company’s team is currently just four folks, though the startup expects that it will double in size this year. The capital it raised in January, but is only talking about now, is making the hiring possible.
 Now, the $1.4 million number is pretty dated. Normally I’d skip over a round so far from the past, but Welcome caught my eye as I’ve recently  written about another HRtech provider,  Sora, and the Welcome deal felt like an illustrative event:  This is how Seed rounds are announced, long after the fact, which makes reporting on Seed-stage trends really hard. Something to keep in mind.
 Welcome is barking up a winsome tree with its product, not only because the offer/offer acceptance process is garbage today — let’s email some PDFs and hand a candidate off between departments! — but because it has seen strong early demand from potential customers. Its service is currently in a private alpha that was a bit oversubscribed, though the company is not yet charging for its service. (Welcome will be a SaaS play, priced on company size, which seems reasonable).
 Past all that, what’s exciting about Welcome is that if it can get a number of customers aboard when it makes it to beta or launch, the company will have placed itself in a position where it can expand in several directions. It could, for example, extend its feature set to help with pre-onboarding or onboarding itself, given that it already knows a new candidate and their new employer. Of course, the startup wants to talk more about what it’s building today, but it’s also fun to look ahead.
 That’s enough on Welcome, we’ll chatter about them again when they formally launch, or share some neat growth metrics. Until then, good luck getting into the alpha.
title: 欢迎募集140万美元以简化招聘流程
title_en: Welcome raises $1.4M to streamline the hiring process

note:
“你们都被赶上了”通知背后的最初想法是为了帮助减少对Instagram令人上瘾的社交应用程序的过度使用。当时，这项功能是Facebook为洞察屏幕时间使用情况而实施的几项功能之一，它与仪表盘一起推出，可以帮助人们查看他们在Facebook和Instagram的应用程序上花费了多少时间，当你达到一天的总时间时，每天都会提醒你，以及让通知保持沉默的工具。
这些变化是一场更大的科技运动的一部分，这场运动质疑社交应用程序是否被设计成太容易上瘾，这可能会对用户的心理健康产生负面影响。大约在这个时候，谷歌和苹果发布了他们自己的屏幕时间管理工具，YouTube也推出了一些功能来跟踪观看视频的时间和鼓励用户休息的提醒。
近几个月来，令人上瘾的中国人拥有的社交视频应用TikTok在美国的增长，加上一场全球流行病，导致许多人改变了早先自我贴在屏幕上的时间限制。反过来，这可能也促使Instagram放弃了自己在屏幕时间管理方面的努力。
作为Facebook社交主导地位的主要竞争对手，TikTok已经因为过于上瘾而受到抨击，这要归功于其先进的算法，它可以快速了解哪些内容吸引了用户的注意力，然后利用这些信息来个性化用户的订阅源。TikTok用户发现，凭借其简短的内容和快捷的界面，他们在这款应用上花费的时间越来越多。根据2019年的数据，美国TikTok用户平均每天打开这款应用8次，平均花费46分钟查看其内容。更新的报告称，这一数字已经增加到每天52分钟，最近的一项研究发现，年轻用户每天可能在这款应用上花费多达80分钟。因此，Instagram现在可能会重新考虑推动用户停止滚动并退出其应用程序的决定也就不足为奇了。
Instagram产品总监罗比·斯坦(Robby Stein)说：“我们的目标是让大家清楚地知道，什么时候你们都被赶上了，这样你们就可以决定如何最好地利用你们的时间。”“我们看到，人们在看到自己的帖子后，还在继续寻找更多他们感兴趣的帖子，所以我们想从中吸取教训，让那些选择这样做的人更容易深入一点。”
该公司还补充说，关注更多账户的人可能一开始就永远不会到达Feed的末尾，也看不到“你们都被抓住了”的信息。
“推荐帖子”功能为Instagram提供了一个新的界面，它可以显示广告，这些广告将与来自你不关注的账户的建议内容一起出现，但Instagram认为你可能会喜欢。对于广告商来说，这意味着人们有更多的机会看到他们的推销。
从商业的角度来看，这种增加的广告空间是更显著的影响。但Instagram决定缩减其数字福利努力，可能会对其应用程序和总体上的更大屏幕时间运动产生更长期的影响。在冠状病毒封锁期间，许多父母已经放弃了对孩子屏幕时间的限制，许多成年人也发现自己最近上网更频繁了。一些专家甚至认为，在我们这个与社会距离遥远的世界里，在线网络增加了价值。这些新的态度可能会导致Instagram的“推荐帖子”功能推出，几乎没有反弹。
note_en:
The original idea behind the “You’re All Caught Up” notice was to help curtail overuse of Instagram’s addictive, social app. At the time, the feature was  one of several Facebook had implemented to provide insights into screen-time usage, having arrived alongside dashboards that would help people see how much time they they’ve spent in Facebook and Instagram’s apps, daily reminders when you hit your total time for the day, and  tools for silencing notifications.
 The changes were part of a larger movement in tech that questioned if social apps had been built to become too addictive, which could have negative consequences on users’ mental health. Around this time, Google and Apple released their own screen time management tools, and  YouTube rolled out features to track time spent watching videos and reminders that encouraged users to take a break.
  In more recent months, the growth of the addictive, Chinese-owned social video app TikTok in the U.S. combined with a global pandemic has seen many people backtracking on their earlier self-imposted screen time limits. This, in turn, may have pushed Instagram to roll back its own efforts in screen time management, too.
 A chief rival to Facebook’s social dominance, TikTok has  already been  slammed for  being too  addictive, thanks to its advanced  algorithms quickly learn what sort of content holds users’ attention then uses that information to personalize users’ feeds. With its short-form content and quick-fire interface, TikTok users are finding themselves spending an increasing amount of time in the app.  According to data from 2019, the average U.S. TikTok user opened the app 8 times per day, and spent 46 minutes, on average, viewing its content. Newer reports says this number has  increased to 52 minutes per day, and  one recent study found that younger users may be spending as much as 80 minutes per day in the app. It’s no surprise, then, that Instagram may now be reconsidering its decision to push users to stop scrolling and leave its app.
 “Our goal is to make it clear when you’re all caught up so you can decide how you want to best use your time,” says Instagram Director of Product, Robby Stein. “We see people continuing to seek out more posts they’re interested in after catching up with their feeds, so we wanted to learn from that and make it easier to go a little deeper for those who choose to do so.”
 The company also adds that people who follow a larger number accounts may never reach the end of their Feed to begin with, and don’t see the “You’re All Caught Up” message.
 The “Suggested Posts” feature gives Instagram a new surface where it can display ads which will appear alongside the suggested content from accounts you don’t follow, but Instagram thinks you might like. For advertisers, that means more chances for people to see their pitch.
 This increased ad space is the more significant impact, from a business perspective. But Instagram’s decision to dial back its digital well-being efforts could have longer-term impacts to its app and the larger screen time movement in general. Already,  many parents have ditched  their children’s screen time limits  amid coronavirus lockdowns, and many adults are  finding themselves online more often these days, as well. Some experts even argue that online networking has increased value in our socially-distanced world. These new attitudes could lead to Instagram’s “Suggested Posts” feature being launched with little to no backlash.
title: Instagram推出“推荐帖子”功能，在您的订阅源末尾找到新的广告空间
title_en: Instagram finds new ad space at the end of your feed with launch of ‘Suggested Posts’ feature

note:
苹果股价在2020年上涨了59%多一点，尽管该公司最新的收益报告显示，营收同比增长了11%。每股收益在同一季度增长了18%，这是一个更令人印象深刻的指标，但与苹果股票实现的价值增长相比，这仍然是一个小得多的结果。
在国内外与新冠肺炎相关的限制措施出台后，许多科技公司立即看到了大幅低点，目前许多科技公司正享受着强劲的市场反弹。市场情绪似乎认为，当全球经济重新全力以赴时，科技公司在其中的市场地位将比以前更强大。在苹果等提供数字服务的公司中，这种说法更是雪上加霜。
科技股今年创下历史新高，纳斯达克综合指数(Nasdaq Composite)最近几天价值超过1.1万点，在令人瞠目结舌的反弹后创下历史新高。
大约三年前，“五大”美国科技公司-Alphabet、亚马逊、微软、苹果和Facebook-的总价值为3万亿美元，这在当时是个大新闻。今天，仅苹果和微软一家的市值就约为3.6万亿美元。
科技巨头的崛起是十年来的故事，他们最近的收益是年度故事。如果市场现在被高估了，当然，这取决于投资大众，即使有很多警告信号表明事情变得有点太热了。
对于初创公司来说，这几乎都是好消息。令人兴奋的科技股公开市场让私募股票显得更有价值，也更令人向往。公开集会有助于推进IPO和收购。所以今天的新闻是，苹果现在足够富有，足以让Croesus感到羞愧，这意味着你友好的当地初创公司可能能够以它喜欢的价格完成下一轮融资。
note_en:
Shares of  Apple   have advanced just over 59% in 2020, despite the company’s  most recent earnings report bearing news of a more modest 11% year-over-year revenue gain. Earnings per-share advanced 18% in the same quarter, a more impressive metric but still a far smaller result than the value increase that Apple’s equity has managed.
 Many technology companies are enjoying a  strong market rebound after seeing  sharp lows in the immediate wake of COVID-19-related restrictions at home and abroad. The market sentiment appears to argue that when the global economy is back to full-power, the market position of tech companies within it will be one of greater strength than before. This argument is doubled among companies that  offer digital services, such as Apple.
  Tech shares have set new all-time highs this year, with the Nasdaq Composite worth more than 11,000 points in recent days, a record tally after a eye-popping rally.
 Around three years ago the “Big Five” American tech companies — Alphabet, Amazon, Microsoft, Apple, and Facebook —  were worth $3 trillion in aggregate, big news at the time. Today Apple and Microsoft are alone worth around $3.6 trillion.
 The rise of the tech giants has been the story of a decade, their recent gains the story of the year. If the market cohort is now overvalued is, of course, up to the investing public  even if warning signs abound that  things are getting a bit too hot.
 For startups, this is nearly all good news. Excited public markets for tech shares make private shares appear more valuable, and desirable. Public rallies can help advance IPOs, and acquisitions. So today’s news is that Apple is now sufficiently rich to shame Croesus, means that your friendly, local startup might be able to close that next round at a price that it likes.
title: 苹果市值达到2万亿美元

note:
苹果花了42年时间才达到1万亿美元的价值。只用了两年多的时间就达到了2万亿美元。
更令人震惊的是：苹果的第二笔1万亿美元全部来自过去21周，而全球经济在冠状病毒大流行期间萎缩的速度比以往任何时候都快。
周三，苹果成为第一家估值达到2万亿美元的美国公司，其股价在早盘交易中攀升1.2%，至467.78美元。对于iPhone、Mac电脑和苹果手表的制造商来说，这是又一个里程碑，巩固了它作为世界上最有价值的上市公司的头衔，并突显了这场流行病对科技巨头来说是多么的财源。
就在3月中旬，在对冠状病毒的担忧导致股市暴跌后，苹果的市值还不到1万亿美元。3月23日，股市跌至今年的最低点，美联储宣布了积极的新措施来安抚投资者。自那以来，股市--尤其是苹果(Apple)、微软(Microsoft)、亚马逊(Amazon)、Alphabet和Facebook的股票--基本上飙升，标准普尔500指数(S&P；500)周二创下新高。
投资者已经向科技巨头投入了数十亿美元，押注它们的巨大规模和力量将成为大流行引发的经济衰退的避难所。根据市场分析公司标准普尔全球(S&P；Global)的数据，自3月23日以来，这五家公司的市值加起来增加了近3万亿美元，几乎相当于标准普尔500指数(S&P；500)下50家最有价值公司(包括伯克希尔哈撒韦(Berkshire Hathaway)、沃尔玛(Walmart)和迪士尼(Disney))的增长总和。仅苹果的估值一天就增加了68亿美元，超过了美国航空公司的价值。
纽约大学(New York University)研究股市的金融学教授阿斯瓦特·达莫达兰(Aswath Damodaran)在谈到蜂拥至大型科技股的投资者时表示：“这已成为新的避险方式。”他说，富有、灵活和数字化的公司正从这场大流行中受益-这就是对科技巨头的描述，并补充说，“这场危机加强了本已强大的实力。”
苹果迅速升至2万亿美元尤其令人吃惊，因为该公司在过去两年并没有做太多新的事情。它只是建立了一个科技行业最有效的赚钱机构之一，它对人们如何沟通、娱乐和购物有着如此牢牢的控制，以至于它不再依赖突破性的发明来维持业务的繁荣。
当苹果在2018年8月首次达到1万亿美元时，它是在数十年的创新之后出现的。这家由史蒂夫·乔布斯(Steve Jobs)和史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克(Steve Wozniak)于1976年创立的公司，大量生产了改变世界的产品，如麦金塔电脑(Macintosh Computer)、iPod、应用商店(App Store)和iPhone。
从那时起，它主要对过去的产品进行了调整，销售苹果手表系列5、AirPods Pro和iPhone11 Pro Max等产品。它还进军流媒体音乐、流媒体电影和电视节目等服务，并提供新闻，销售订阅服务。
在首席执行官蒂姆·库克(Tim Cook)的领导下，苹果近年来最重要的创新可以说是其创造利润的能力几乎无与伦比。库克建立了一条复杂的全球供应链，生产了数十亿台设备--其中大部分在中国组装--并投入到一条产品线上，该产品线旨在将客户锁定在其生态系统中，这样他们每隔几年就会购买新设备，并每月支付费用才能使用苹果的数字服务套件。
尽管苹果的规模很大，但它也通过从在iPhone应用程序上运营业务的公司赚取更多资金来实现增长，这招致了指责，称其从一些应用程序收入中抽成30%是不公平的。
这场流行病迫使人们虚拟地工作、学习和社交，这只会让这家硅谷公司的业务变得更加根深蒂固。从4月到6月，尽管苹果公司因病毒关闭了许多零售店，但它公布的利润为112.5亿美元，比一年前增长了12%。它增加了每一种产品在世界各地的销量。
苹果首席财务官卢卡·马埃斯特里(Luca Maestri)上个月在接受采访时说，“我们的产品和服务与客户的生活非常相关，在某些情况下，在大流行期间甚至比以往任何时候都更相关。”
尽管如此，马埃斯特里驳斥了大流行对商业有利的说法。他说，如果没有它，苹果可能会多赚数十亿美元。
在该公司最近一次财报电话会议上，库克说，“我们对繁荣没有零和博弈。”他补充说：“我们专注于做大蛋糕，确保我们的成功不仅仅是我们的成功，我们所做、所做或所做的一切都是为了为他人创造机会。”
亚马逊(Amazon)、微软(Microsoft)、Facebook和拥有谷歌(Google)和YouTube的Alphabet也在大流行期间继续赚取数十亿美元。他们的超大影响力在过去一年里引起了密切关注，包括上个月在国会对这两家公司的四名首席执行官进行的两党质询。
罗德岛州民主党众议员大卫·西西林(David Cicilline)是众议院小组委员会主席，该委员会正在调查科技巨头如何挥舞自己的权力。他在听证会上警告称，这些公司已经变得过于强大。
他说：“他们口授条件、发号施令、颠覆整个行业、激起恐惧的能力代表了私营政府的力量。”“尽管很难相信，但我们的经济可能会比以前更加集中和巩固地走出这场危机。”
上周，当苹果将热门游戏“堡垒之夜”从其应用商店下架时，苹果对其应用商店的影响力成为聚光灯下的焦点。生产堡垒之夜的Epic Games随后在联邦法院起诉苹果，指控该公司强迫开发者使用其支付系统，违反了反垄断法。
苹果还运用了另一个强大的工具来提高估值，让投资者和高管致富：股票回购。自该公司市值触及1万亿美元以来，已向股东返还1,756亿美元，其中包括1,410亿美元的股票回购。自2012年以来，苹果已经回购了超过3600亿美元的自有股票，是所有公司中回购最多的，并宣布了至少再花费数百亿美元购买苹果股票的计划。
苹果利用特朗普政府2017年的税法，将其曾经在海外持有的2,520亿美元中的大部分带回，此后苹果加大了回购力度。(根据华盛顿的研究机构税收与经济政策研究所(Institute On Taxation And Economic Policy)的数据，这项法律在此举中为它节省了430亿美元的税款。)。苹果拥有1,940亿美元的现金和债券。
回购股票通常会提高公司的股价，部分原因是它减少了待售股票的总数。批评人士认为，这也加剧了不平等，因为它主要是让富有的投资者和公司自己的高管致富，他们往往是大股东，就像苹果的情况一样。高管和一些经济学家表示，将多余的现金返还给股东比袖手旁观要好。
苹果是第二家市值达到2万亿美元的上市公司。沙特阿拉伯国有石油公司沙特阿美去年12月上市，并短暂突破2万亿美元大关。在苹果上个月超越它之前，它一直是世界上最有价值的公司。
其他人则在争先恐后地很快达到2万亿美元大关。接下来可能达到这一里程碑的候选人是谁？微软、亚马逊和Alphabet。
note_en:
It took Apple 42 years to  reach $1 trillion in value. It took it just two more years to get to $2 trillion.
 Even more stunning: All of Apple’s second $1 trillion came in the past 21 weeks, while  the global economy  shrank faster than ever before in the coronavirus pandemic.
 On Wednesday Apple became the first U.S. company to hit a $2 trillion valuation when its shares climbed 1.2 percent to $467.78 in morning trading. It was another milestone for the maker of iPhones, Mac computers and Apple Watches, cementing its title as the world’s most valuable public company and punctuating how  the pandemic has been a bonanza for the tech giants.
 As recently as mid-March, Apple’s value was under $1 trillion after the stock market plunged over fears of the coronavirus. On March 23, the stock market’s nadir this year, the Federal Reserve announced  aggressive new measures to calm investors. Since then, the stock market — and particularly the stocks of Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet and Facebook — largely soared, with the  S&P 500 hitting a new high on Tuesday.
 Investors have poured billions of dollars into the tech behemoths, betting that their  immense size and power would serve as refuges from the pandemic-induced  recession. Together, those five companies’ value has swelled by almost $3 trillion since March 23, nearly the same growth as the S&P 500’s next 50 most valuable companies combined, including Berkshire Hathaway, Walmart and Disney, according to S&P Global, the market analytics firm. Apple’s valuation alone rose by $6.8 billion a day, more than the value of American Airlines.
 “It’s become the new flight to safety,” Aswath Damodaran, a New York University finance professor who studies the stock market, said of investors flocking to Big Tech. Companies that are rich, flexible and digital are benefiting in the pandemic — and that describes the tech Goliaths, he said, adding, “This crisis has strengthened what was already a strong hand.”
  Apple’s rapid rise to $2 trillion is particularly astonishing because the company has not done much new in the past two years. It has simply built one of the tech industry’s most effective moneymakers, which has such a firm grip over how people communicate, entertain themselves and shop that it no longer relies on groundbreaking inventions to keep the business humming.
 When Apple first reached  $1 trillion in August 2018, it came after decades of innovation. The company, founded in 1976 by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak, churned out world-changing products like the Macintosh computer, the iPod, the App Store and the iPhone.
 Since then, it has mostly tweaked past creations, selling gadgets with names like the Apple Watch Series 5, the AirPods Pro and the iPhone 11 Pro Max. It has also pushed into services such as streaming music, streaming movies and TV programs and  providing news, selling subscriptions for them.
  Under its chief executive, Tim Cook, Apple’s most important innovation in recent years has arguably been its nearly unrivaled ability to generate profits. Mr. Cook has built a sophisticated global supply chain to produce billions of devices — most assembled in China — and leaned into a product line designed to lock customers into its ecosystem so they buy new gadgets every few years and pay monthly fees to use Apple’s suite of digital services.
 Apple has also grown despite its size by extracting more money from the companies that run businesses on iPhone apps,  drawing accusations that  its 30-percent cut of some app revenues is unfair.
 The Silicon Valley company’s business has been only  further entrenched by the pandemic, which has forced people to work, learn and socialize virtually. From April through June, even as Apple  shuttered many of its retail stores because of the virus, it posted $11.25 billion in profits, up 12 percent from a year ago. It increased its sales of every product and in every part of the world.
 “Our products and services are very relevant to our customers’ lives, and in some cases, even more during the pandemic than ever before,” Luca Maestri, Apple’s finance chief, said in an interview last month.
 Still, Mr. Maestri disputed that the pandemic has been good for business. Apple would have made billions of dollars more without it, he said.
 In the company’s most recent earnings call, Mr. Cook said, “We do not have a zero-sum approach to prosperity.” He added, “We are focused on growing the pie, making sure our success isn’t just our success and that everything we make, build or do is geared toward creating opportunities for others.”
  Amazon, Microsoft, Facebook and Alphabet, which owns Google and YouTube, have also continued  raking in billions of dollars amid the pandemic. Their outsized influence has attracted intense scrutiny over the past year, including  a bipartisan grilling of four of the companies’ chief executives in Congress last month.
 Representative David Cicilline, Democrat of Rhode Island and chairman of the House subcommittee that is investigating how the tech giants are brandishing their power, warned at the hearing that the companies had grown too powerful.
 “Their ability to dictate terms, call the shots, upend entire sectors and inspire fear represent the powers of a private government,” he said. “As hard as it is to believe, it is possible that our economy will emerge from this crisis even more concentrated and consolidated than before.”
 Last week, Apple’s power over its App Store was in the spotlight when it  booted the popular game Fortnite from its store. Epic Games, which makes Fortnite, then sued Apple in federal court, accusing the company of violating antitrust laws by forcing developers to use its payment systems.
 Apple has also wielded another powerful tool to boost its valuation and enrich its investors and executives:  stock buybacks. Since the company’s value hit $1 trillion, it has returned $175.6 billion to shareholders, including $141 billion in stock buybacks. Apple has repurchased more than $360 billion of its own shares since 2012, by far the most of any company, and has announced plans to spend at least tens of billions of dollars more on Apple stock.
 Apple has increased its buybacks since it used the Trump administration’s 2017 tax law to  bring back most of the $252 billion it had once held abroad. (The law saved it $43 billion in taxes on the move, according to the Institute on Taxation and Economic Policy, a research group in Washington.) Apple has $194 billion in cash and bonds.
 Buying back stock generally increases a company’s share price, in part because it reduces the total number of shares for sale. Critics have  argued that it also increases inequality because it mostly enriches wealthy investors and the company’s own executives, who are often large shareholders, as is the case with Apple. Executives and some economists said that  returning excess cash to shareholders is better than sitting on it.
 Apple is the second publicly traded company to hit $2 trillion. Saudi Aramco, Saudi Arabia’s state-owned oil company, went public in December and briefly exceeded the $2 trillion mark. It remained the world’s most valuable company until Apple surpassed it last month.
 Others are vying to reach the $2 trillion mark soon. The candidates likely to hit that milestone next?  Microsoft,  Amazon and  Alphabet.
title: 苹果现在价值2万亿美元，突显了大型科技公司的掌控

note:
Instagram今天通过推出“推荐帖子”来扩展其订阅源。这些帖子来自你没有关注的账号，它们会在你的帖子结束后出现，让你可以选择继续滚动Instagram的建议。到目前为止，订阅源完全是由用户的偏好和他们关注的人决定的。
在过去的几年里，Instagram会在用户看到订阅源结束时向他们显示一条消息，这意味着他们会看到过去两天里关注的人发布的每一篇帖子。有了推荐的帖子，他们将可以选择继续滚动通过该标记以获得更多内容。(这条信息将仍然存在，同时还会有重新访问旧帖子的选项。)。
建议的帖子将不会与“浏览”中显示的帖子相同。Instagram Home的产品主管朱利安·古特曼(Julian Gutman)表示，它们将与人们已经关注的内容相关，而Explore的目标是将人们引导到邻近的内容。他以空间内容为例，他定期在他的feed上关注和接触这些内容。推荐的帖子可能是他没有关注的人发的一张新的太空照片，而他的探索页面可能包含更广泛的与物理相关的帖子。
古特曼拒绝就人们每天实际到达订阅结束的频率发表评论。在推荐的帖子中会有广告，目前只有发布到网格上的照片和视频才会显示出来，这意味着你不会看到被戏弄的IGTV或Reels内容。
与两年前Instagram在屏幕时间上的立场相比，此举感觉是一个重大逆转。在推出订阅结束通知时，该公司前首席执行官兼联合创始人凯文·希斯特罗姆(Kevin Systrom)将其定位为考虑到用户心理健康而做出的决定。他当时表示，该公司正在开发“工具，帮助Instagram社区更多地了解他们在Instagram上花费的时间”，并补充说，“任何时间都应该是积极和有意的。”但推荐的帖子很容易让人们在这款应用上停留更长时间。
古特曼说，这款产品的设计初衷并不是为了让人们粘在手机上。
他说：“我认为，对我们来说，这确实来自于几年前的一种愿景，那就是人们真的把Feed视为自己感兴趣的地方，随着机器学习的进步和我们的能力，你可以更容易地看到更多每天在Instagram上发布的帖子，并真正将这种个性化的相关性带给你。”“因此，我们只是想让人们在看到订阅源的末尾时，能很容易地看到那些(相关内容)。这才是真正的动机，让你更容易深入挖掘你的兴趣。“。
推荐的帖子也让人感觉Instagram是在借鉴TikTok的做法，TikTok将用户推入永无止境的内容源源不断的馈送中。这也是这款应用如此成功的部分原因。当然，拉长Feed也给了Instagram更多的广告空间，使其能够进一步盈利。这对Instagram来说是双赢的，即使这意味着用户最终在这款应用上花费的时间可能会比以往任何时候都多。
note_en:
Instagram is expanding its feed today with the launch of “suggested posts.” These posts, from accounts you don’t follow, will show up after you’ve reached the end of your feed and give you the option to keep scrolling with Instagram’s suggestions. Up until now, the feed has been entirely determined by users’ preferences and the people they follow.
 For the past couple of years, Instagram  has shown users a message when they reach the end of their feeds, meaning they’ve seen every post over the past two days from people they follow. With suggested posts, they’ll have the option to keep scrolling past that marker for more content. (That message will still be there along with the option to revisit old posts.)
 The suggested posts won’t be the same ones that show up in Explore. They’ll be related to the content that people already follow, whereas Explore aims to point people toward adjacent content, says Julian Gutman, head of product at Instagram Home. He used space content, which he follows and engages with on his feed regularly, as an example. A suggested post might be a new space photo from someone he doesn’t follow, whereas his Explore page might contain posts related to physics more broadly.
 Gutman declined to comment on how often people actually reach the end of their feeds on a daily basis. There will be ads in suggested posts, and only photos and videos posted to the grid will show up for now, meaning you won’t see teased IGTV or Reels content.
 The move feels like a major reversal from Instagram’s stance two years ago on screen time. When it launched the end of feed notification, the company’s former CEO and co-founder, Kevin Systrom, positioned it as a decision made with users’ mental health in mind. He said at the time that the company was working on “tools that will help the Instagram community know more about the time they spent on Instagram,“ adding that “any time should be positive and intentional.” But suggested posts could easily keep people on the app for longer.
 Gutman says the product wasn’t designed to keep people glued to their phones.
 “I think for us this really came from a vision a couple years ago around people really seeing feed as a place for their interests, and with the improvement in machine learning and our ability to kind of make it easier for you to see some more of those posts that are on Instagram every day, and really bring that personalized relevance to you,” he says. “So we just want to make it really easy for people to see that [relevant content] when they get to the end of their feed. That’s really the motivation here, make it easier for you to go deeper on your interest.”
 The suggested posts also feel like Instagram is taking a cue from TikTok, which throws users into a continuous feed of content that never ends. It’s part of what’s made the app so successful. Of course, lengthening the feed also gives Instagram more ad space, allowing it to further monetize. It’s a win-win for Instagram, even if it means users will potentially end up spending more time than ever in the app.
title: Instagram推出了“建议帖子”，在用户到达订阅源的末尾时出现，提供相关内容，而不是Explore订阅源的相邻内容
title_en: Instagram rolls out “suggested posts”, appearing when users reach the end of their feed, offering related content unlike Explore feed's adjacent content

今年科技平台更有希望的发展之一是，他们在消除与新冠肺炎大流行相关的错误信息方面进行了投资。Facebook、YouTube和Twitter都相对较快地承认了COVID恶作剧所代表的威胁，并已努力将其从他们的网络中清除。然而，这些错误信息政策的执行有时落后于公司的公开声明。在5月份被平台发现并移除之前，一段名为“Plandhemic”的反疫苗挑逗道具吸引了数百万的浏览量。更令人担忧的是，在Facebook控制住它之前，仅在Facebook上就有2000万人看到了一条推动虚假COVID治疗的新宣传。
周一，错误信息研究人员本·德克尔(Ben Decker)警告称，一部真正的“Plandic”续集即将上映。原版视频的制作人承诺，第二部将于周二首播，并在Facebook上进行了至少887次推广，拥有数十万粉丝的页面上进行了至少887次推广。这段新视频很可能会被撤下，这一事实成为围绕它的营销活动的一部分。剩下的就是看看当它真正上线时会发生什么。
有些令人惊讶的是，LinkedIn最先采取了行动，在视频首播之前就将一个宣传视频的关键账户从其平台上删除，理由是该账户违反了服务条款。当它这样做的时候-以可笑的坏名字Plandhemic：Indoctoration-纽约时报的Davey Alba很有帮助地记录了它包含的各种错误信息。简而言之：沼泽标准的反Vaxx阴谋论，根植于少数影子精英为了从中获利而让世界陷入停滞的观念。如果你以前都听过，很容易就会不屑一顾。但我们也知道，这些东西正像野火一样在网上蔓延，特别是在Facebook的群组中，而反Vaxx情绪可能会减缓大流行的恢复，并限制我们获得群体免疫的前景。
录像带上传之后发生了什么？阿迪·罗伯逊(Adi Robertson)在The Verge上报道了这个故事：
社交媒体网站正试图阻止Plandemic：Indoctoration的传播，这是Plandheme阴谋视频的后续视频，内容是关于新型冠状病毒的。正如NBC新闻记者布兰迪·扎德罗兹尼(Brandy Zadrozny)所指出的那样，Facebook阻止用户转发今天早些时候上传到外部网站的新视频的链接。Twitter没有屏蔽该视频链接，但它会将点击该链接的用户发送到一个警告屏幕，称该链接“可能是垃圾邮件或不安全的”。
Twitter向The Verge证实，它是在警告人们，而不是屏蔽链接；该公司将根据具体情况评估任何直接上传的短片，并可能删除它认为危险的任何错误信息。发布这段视频的流媒体频道London Real报道称，该视频在首播前被LinkedIn暂停播放。根据CrowdTangle的数据，伦敦皇马最初发布的链接到这段视频的帖子在Facebook上有大约5.3万次互动。这段视频的转载版本可以在YouTube上找到，但目前点击量不到200次。
Indoctoration仍有可能在社交平台上找到新的生命。但看起来，这一次平台似乎在很大程度上通过了测试：他们实时识别出视频违反了他们的标准，停止了托管，并阻止用户分享。2020年，成功的内容政策是这样的：你不能阻止所有不好的东西都被上传，但你可以迅速识别出来，并采取有效的行动。上个月，这样的视频获得了2000万的点击量。今天，在YouTube上，它的浏览量不到200。像这样的工作很难，但也是可能的，我认为当它做得正确的时候，把它叫出来是很重要的。
当然，你可以说我在用微弱的赞扬贬低平台。正如凯文·鲁斯(Kevin Roose)所指出的那样，Indoctoration是在它到来之前887次宣布的罕见的错误信息。这是关于首映日期的错误信息。与上个月的内容审核灾难形成鲜明对比的是，上个月的内容审核灾难本质上是在新闻发布会现场直播时说的一堆废话。看看这两个视频的情况，就不难理解为什么Facebook、YouTube和Twitter在周二比7月份更容易。
而且Indoctoration可以说没有以前的COVID恶作剧那么危险，因为它并没有试图给出医疗建议，而且主要是对比尔·盖茨密谋反对你的想法的延伸即兴表演。这就是为什么这段视频在技术上仍然被允许在Facebook上播放的原因之一-它只是被置于事实核查警告之下，不会在Feed中得到广泛传播。
一位发言人告诉我：“鉴于之前的Plandemic视频违反了我们的COVID错误信息政策，我们从我们的服务中阻止了对该域名的访问。”“这段最新的视频包含新冠肺炎声称，我们的事实核查合作伙伴一再给出虚假评级，所以我们减少了它的传播，并增加了一个警告标签，向任何看到它的人展示他们的发现。”
YouTube告诉我，就其自身而言，它几乎没有看到上传Indoctoration的尝试，并且正在删除完整的上传，因为它认为这些上传违反了它关于COVID错误信息的政策。该公司告诉我，如果其他上传内容包含原始视频片段，将根据具体情况进行评估。
我想澄清的是，社交平台上关于健康错误信息的问题比单一内容的降级要大得多。信息生态系统显然受到了污染，人们正在遭受痛苦。亚当·萨塔里亚诺(Adam Satariano)本周在“纽约时报”(New York Times)上探讨了这个问题：
上周，研究人员表示，今年前三个月，全球至少有800人死亡，另有数千人住院，原因是网上毫无根据地声称，摄入高浓度酒精会杀死病毒。他们的发现是基于对网络上流传的谣言的研究，发表在“美国热带医学与卫生杂志”(American Journal Of Tropical Medicine And Hygiene)上。
医生的挫折感充斥着Facebook的群组和在线论坛。美国医学会和其他代表医生的团体表示，网上传播的虚假信息正在损害公众对这种疾病的反应。世界卫生组织(World Health Organization)正在开发方法来衡量与病毒相关的错误信息在网上的危害，该组织在7月份的两周多时间里主办了一次在线会议，与医生、公共卫生专家和互联网研究人员一起讨论如何解决这个问题。
但这些平台周二的行动向我们表明，取得进展是可能的。他们知道如何做正确的事情，而且他们能做到。尤其是当坏人告诉他们去哪里找的时候。
下降趋势：Facebook在4月份放弃了数千加仑的钻井液，就在俄勒冈州海岸附近的海底。该公司在为一条海底电信电缆建造着陆点时，遇到了意想不到的障碍。
⭐一些司法部工作人员担心，尽管司法部长威廉·巴尔今年夏天推动提起诉讼，但针对谷歌的案件还没有准备好。他们说，该部门需要更多时间来考虑他们拥有的数百万页文件是否足以在法庭上赢得一场反垄断诉讼。“华尔街日报”的布伦特·肯德尔报道：
数十名政府反垄断律师正在调查这家搜索巨头是否利用其主导地位扼杀竞争。知情人士说，一个小组专注于谷歌的搜索实践，其一些成员表示，他们认为围绕这些问题建立的案件存在漏洞。有关司法部法律理论的细节无法获知。
另一个团队正在研究谷歌的在线广告业务，该公司在在线出版商和广告商之间复杂链条的每一个环节都拥有业界领先的工具。知情人士说，从事这方面调查的一些律师还没有准备好向前推进，因为他们仍在理清谷歌这部分业务带来的新的复杂问题，以及它对数字生态系统中的许多公司有何影响。
共和党控制的参议院小组发布的一份庞大的报告证实了俄罗斯试图干预2016年大选的关键事实。俄罗斯政府试图破坏2016年大选以帮助特朗普获胜，特朗普顾问圈子中的一些成员对美国对手的帮助持开放态度。(Mark Mazzetti和Nicholas Fandos/纽约时报)。
由前反特朗普共和党人发起的政治行动委员会林肯项目(Lincoln Project)一直在窃取表情包，网络左翼对此感到不满。它是按照杰瑞媒体(Jerry Media)等营销集团和@theFate jemish等账户流行的策略运营的。(Makena Kelly/The Verge)。
Facebook拒绝了冈比亚提出的帮助其调查缅甸种族灭绝事件的请求，称这一请求“非常广泛”，而且“过于侵扰或负担过重”。今年6月，这个西非国家向美国联邦法院提交了一份申请，要求从Facebook获得信息，以帮助它在国际法院(International Court Of Justice)追究缅甸的责任。文/马修·史密斯/时代周刊。
由反疫苗活动家小罗伯特·F·肯尼迪(Robert F.Kennedy Jr.)创立的组织儿童健康保护(Children‘s Health Defense)正在起诉Facebook拒绝广告，并揭穿了有关疫苗和5G网络的说法。该投诉针对Facebook、其首席执行官马克·扎克伯格，以及事实核查组织PolitiFact、Science Feedback和Poynter Institute。这是极其愚蠢的。(Adi Robertson/The Verge)
Epic Games在对这家科技巨头提起诉讼后，正在与其他科技公司接触，商谈组建苹果批评者联盟的事宜。Sonos和Spotify都在考虑加入该集团。(尼克·温菲尔德和亚历克斯·希思/The Information)。
Epic Games表示，苹果威胁要将其从苹果开发者计划中移除，威胁要破坏每一款使用其广受欢迎的虚拟引擎的游戏。苹果回应称：“如果他们提交应用程序的更新，使其恢复到符合他们同意的、适用于所有开发者的指导方针，EpicePic为自己造成的问题很容易解决。”(Sam Byford/The Verge)。
在美中关系下滑的情况下，苹果在中国面临多年来最严峻的环境。苹果在没有政府许可证和当地合作伙伴的情况下在中国运营App Store，但它不得不在安抚中国官员和不在国内引发反弹之间走一条越来越困难的线。文/《资讯报》记者马伟民(Wayne Ma/The Information)。
研究人员和公共卫生官员表示，隐私友好的自愿新冠肺炎跟踪应用程序尚未对大流行产生明显影响。在韩国和科威特推出的更激进的应用程序可能更有效，但它们也引入了隐私问题。(Craig Timberg，Steve Hendrix，Min Joo Kim和Fiona Weber-Steinhaus/华盛顿邮报)。
迈阿密警方使用Clearview AI的面部识别技术逮捕了一名抗议者，他们称这名抗议者参与了5月30日的对峙。涉案人员被逮捕，并被指控殴打一名警察。他们已提出无罪抗辩。(Connie Fossi和Phil Prazan/NBC)。
特朗普的一名前工作人员曾参与政府备受争议的家庭分居政策，后来加入谷歌，目前正从这家科技巨头休假，以支持乔·拜登(Joe Biden)的竞选活动。迈尔斯·泰勒周一出现在一则反特朗普的广告中，并支持拜登竞选总统。(Jennifer Elias/CNBC)。
⭐甲骨文正在洽谈收购TikTok，此举将对微软构成挑战。这两家公司都远远领先于其他表示有兴趣的美国公司。值得注意的是，甲骨文创始人拉里·埃里森(Larry Ellison)在过去四年的大部分时间里都在讨好特朗普。CNBC的亚历克斯·谢尔曼有独家新闻：
甲骨文没有面向消费者的社交媒体或视频业务。从理论上讲，甲骨文可以利用TikTok收集的客户数据来改进其营销产品，但斥资数百亿美元收购一家消费者社交媒体公司对该公司来说将是一个重大的背离。随着亚马逊网络服务(Amazon Web Services)在云计算领域占据主导地位，微软Azure和谷歌云(Google Cloud)紧随其后，甲骨文一直在努力寻找新的增长途径。甲骨文第四财季营收下滑6%至104亿美元。甲骨文有收购欲强的历史，但近年来在大型交易上放慢了脚步。
TikTok开通了一个网站和Twitter账户，以实时处理错误信息。该公司在网站上表示：“让我们澄清事实。”“TikTok从未向中国政府提供过任何美国用户数据，如果被要求，它也不会这样做.”我的基本感觉是，如果你必须说“让我们澄清事实”，你已经输掉了这场争论。这是Facebook旧版的2020版“难题”博客，似乎对品牌认知的影响程度大致相同。(内森·克鲁克斯/彭博社)。
广告商正在重新考虑在TikTok上做广告的计划，因为美国可能会禁止他们在TikTok上做广告。对隐私的担忧也促使一些人避免使用这款应用。(Anissa Gardizy/The Information)。
TikTok在2019年推出了一个创作者大使计划，以帮助一小部分有影响力的人在这款应用上增加粉丝。该计划有几个额外的好处，比如所有费用的旅行，与品牌的联系，以及使用新的应用程序功能。趁你还能享受到这些好处，炒作房子吧！(Amanda Perelli/Business Insider)。
TikTokers发布视频，取笑他们支持特朗普的父母。因此，至少有一件好事已经从隔离中走出来了。(卡迪亚·戈巴/BuzzFeed)。
Snapchat正在测试一项功能，允许用户与平台外的朋友分享快照原创、节目和出版商的故事。这是该公司之前为让用户在其应用程序之外分享故事而做出的努力的扩展。(Sara Fischer/Axios)。
Oculus很快将要求所有虚拟现实耳机用户使用Facebook账户注册。这家Facebook旗下的公司表示，将从10月份开始取消对单独Oculus账户的支持。(Adi Robertson/The Verge)。

The Interface is a daily column and newsletter about the intersection of social media and democracy. Subscribe  here.
 One of the more hopeful developments at tech platforms this year has been their investment in removing misinformation related to the COVID-19 pandemic. Facebook, YouTube, and Twitter were all relatively quick to acknowledge the threat that COVID hoaxes represent, and have worked to purge it from their networks. Enforcement of those misinformation policies has sometimes lagged behind the companies’ public statements, though. A piece of anti-vaccination agitprop catchily titled “Plandemic”  racked up millions of views before it was spotted and taken down by the platforms in May. More worryingly,  a new piece of propaganda pushing a phony COVID cure was seen by 20 million people on Facebook alone before the company got it under control.
 On Monday, the misinformation researcher Ben Decker warned that  a true “Plandemic” sequel was coming. Makers of the original video promised that a second installment would premiere Tuesday, and promoted it at least 887 times on Facebook, from pages with hundreds of thousands of followers. The fact that the new video was likely to be taken down became part of the marketing campaign around it. All that remained was to see what happened when it actually went live.
 Somewhat surprisingly, LinkedIn acted first,  removing a key account promoting the video from its platform for violating the terms of service before it could even premiere. And when it did — under the hilariously bad name  Plandemic: Indoctornation — the New York Times’ Davey Alba  helpfully chronicled the various pieces of misinformation it contains. In short: bog-standard anti-vaxx conspiracy theories, rooted in the notion that a handful of shadowy elites brought the world to a standstill in order to profit from it. If you’ve heard it all before, it’s easy to dismiss. But we also know that this stuff is spreading like wildfire online,  particularly within Facebook groups, and that anti-vaxx sentiment could slow the pandemic recovery and limit our prospects of herd immunity.
 So what happened after the video went up? Adi Robertson  picks up the story at  The Verge:
 Social media sites are trying to stop the spread of  Plandemic: Indoctornation, a follow-up to the  Plandemic conspiracy video about the novel coronavirus. As   NBC News reporter Brandy Zadrozny noted, Facebook blocks users from reposting a link to the new video, which was uploaded to an external site earlier today. Twitter doesn’t block the video link, but it sends users who click it to a warning screen, saying that the link is “potentially spammy or unsafe.”
 Twitter confirmed to  The Verge that it’s warning people rather than blocking the link; the company will evaluate any short clips that are directly uploaded on a case-by-case basis and may remove any that it deems dangerous misinformation. Streaming channel London Real, which posted the video, reported that it was suspended by LinkedIn before its premiere. According to CrowdTangle, London Real’s original post linking to the video has about 53,000 interactions on Facebook. A reposted version of the video can be found on YouTube, but it currently has under 200 views.
 There’s still some possibility that  Indoctornation will find new life on the social platforms. But it appears that for the most part, this time platforms passed the test: they identified the video as being in violation of their standards in real time, stopped hosting it and prevented users from sharing it. In 2020, this is what successful content policy looks like: you can’t prevent every bad thing from ever being uploaded, but you can identify it quickly and take effective action. Last month, a video like this got 20 million views. Today, on YouTube, it got fewer than 200. Work like this is hard, but it’s also possible, and I think it’s important to call it out when it’s done correctly.
 Of course, you could say I’m damning the platforms with faint praise. As  Kevin Roose notes,  Indoctornation was the rare piece of misinformation to be announced 887 times ahead of its arrival. It was misinformation with a  premiere  date. Contrast that with last month’s content moderation disaster, which was essentially a bunch of nonsense that was uttered during a live stream of a press conference. Looking at the circumstances of both videos, it’s not hard to understand why Facebook, YouTube, and Twitter had an easier time on Tuesday than they did in July.
 And  Indoctornation is arguably less dangerous than previous COVID hoaxes, since it doesn’t attempt to give medical advice and is mostly an extended riff on the idea that Bill Gates is conspiring against you. That’s one reason why the video is still technically  allowed on Facebook — it’s just placed under a fact-checking warning, and will not get wide distribution in the feed.
 “Given the previous Plandemic video violated our COVID misinformation policies, we blocked access to that domain from our services,” a spokesman told me. “This latest video contains COVID-19 claims that our fact-checking partners have repeatedly rated false so we have reduced its distribution and added a warning label showing their findings to anyone who sees it.”
 YouTube told me that for its part, it had seen few attempts to upload  Indoctornation, and is removing full uploads as it sees them for violating its policies around COVID misinformation. If other uploads contain segments of the original video, they’ll be evaluated on a case-by-case basis, the company told me.
 I want to be clear that the issues around health misinformation on social platforms are much bigger than the down-ranking of a single piece of content. The information ecosystem is clearly polluted, and people are suffering.  Adam Satariano took a look at the issue this week at the  New York Times:
 Last week, researchers said that at least 800 people worldwide died in the first three months of the year, and thousands more were hospitalized, from unfounded claims online that ingesting highly concentrated alcohol would kill the virus. Their findings, based on studying rumors circulating on the web, were  published in the American Journal of Tropical Medicine and Hygiene.
 Doctors’ frustrations fill Facebook groups and online forums. The American Medical Association and other groups representing doctors say the false information spreading online is harming the public health response to the disease. The World Health Organization is developing methods to measure the harm of virus-related misinformation online, and over two weeks in July the group hosted  an online conference with doctors, public health experts and internet researchers about how to address the problem.
 But the platforms’ actions on Tuesday showed us that progress is possible. They know how to do the right thing, and they can. Especially when the bad guys tell them where to look.
   Trending down:   Facebook abandoned thousands of gallons of drilling fluid under the ocean floor just off the coast of Oregon in April. The company was constructing a landing site for an undersea telecommunications cable when when it hit an unexpected snag.
  ⭐  Some Justice Department staffers are worried the case against  Google isn’t ready, despite  Attorney General William Barr pushing for a lawsuit this summer. They say the department needs more time to consider whether the millions of pages of documents they have are enough to win an antitrust case in court. Brent Kendall at  The Wall Street Journal has the story:
 Dozens of government antitrust lawyers are on teams investigating whether the search giant has used its dominance to stifle competition. One group is focused on Google’s search practices, and some of its members have voiced the belief that there are vulnerabilities in a case built around those issues, people familiar with the matter said. Details about the Justice Department’s legal theories couldn’t be learned.
 Another team is examining  Google’s online advertising business, where the company owns industry-leading tools at every link in the complex chain between online publishers and advertisers. Some attorneys working on that aspect of the probe aren’t ready to move forward because they are still untangling the new and complex issues raised by that part of Google’s business and how it affects the many companies in the digital ecosystem, the people said.
 A sprawling report released by a Republican-controlled Senate panel confirmed key facts about Russia’s attempted meddling in the 2016 election. The Russian government tried to sabotage the 2016 election to help Trump win, and some members of Trump’s circle of advisers were open to the help from an American adversary. (Mark Mazzetti and Nicholas Fandos /  The New York Times)
 The Lincoln Project, a political action committee started by former anti- Trump Republicans, has been stealing memes, and the online left isn’t happy about it. It’s operating under a playbook popularized by marketing groups like Jerry Media and accounts like @thefatjewish. (Makena Kelly /  The Verge)
 Facebook is rejecting a request from The Gambia to help it investigate the genocide in Myanmar, saying the request was “extraordinarily broad” as well as “unduly intrusive or burdensome.” In June, the West African nation filed an application in US federal court seeking information from Facebook that would help it hold Myanmar accountable at the International Court of Justice. (Matthew Smith /  Time)
 Children’s Health Defense, a group founded by anti-vaccine activist Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is suing  Facebook for rejecting ads and labeling debunked claims about vaccines and 5G networks. The complaint is against Facebook, its CEO  Mark Zuckerberg, and the fact-checking organizations PolitiFact, Science Feedback, and Poynter Institute. It is extremely dumb. (Adi Robertson /  The Verge)
 Epic Games is reaching out to other tech companies about forming forming a coalition of  Apple critics, after filing a lawsuit against the tech giant. Both  Sonos and  Spotify are considering joining the group. (Nick Wingfield and Alex Heath /  The Information)
 Epic Games said  Apple is threatening to remove it from the Apple Developer Program, threatening to break every game that uses its popular Unreal Engine. Apple responded: “The problem Epic has created for itself is one that can easily be remedied if they submit an update of their app that reverts it to comply with the guidelines they agreed to and which apply to all developers.” (Sam Byford /  The Verge)
 Apple is facing its toughest environment in China in years amid the decline of US-China relations. Apple operates the App Store in China without government licenses and local partners, but it’s having to walk an increasingly difficult line between placating Chinese officials and not triggering a backlash back home. (Wayne Ma /  The Information)
 Privacy-friendly, voluntary COVID-19 tracking apps have not had a demonstrable impact on the pandemic, researchers and public health officials say. The more aggressive apps introduced in South Korea and Kuwait may have been more effective, but they also introduced privacy concerns. (Craig Timberg, Steve Hendrix, Min Joo Kim and Fiona Weber-Steinhaus /  The Washington Post)
 Miami police used  Clearview AI’s facial recognition technology to arrest a protestor who they say was involved in a standoff on May 30th. The person in question was arrested and charged with battery on a police officer. They have pleaded not guilty. (Connie Fossi and Phil Prazan / NBC)
 A former  Trump staffer who worked on the administration’s controversial family separation policies, and later joined  Google, is on leave from the tech giant to support Joe Biden’s campaign. Miles Taylor appeared in an anti-Trump ad Monday and backed Biden for president. (Jennifer Elias / CNBC)
  ⭐  Oracle is in talks to acquire  TikTok, a move that would challenge  Microsoft. Both companies are far ahead of other US firms that have expressed interest. It’s notable because Oracle founder Larry Ellison has spent much of the past four years sucking up to Trump. Alex Sherman at CNBC has the scoop:
 Oracle doesn’t have a consumer-facing social media or video business. In theory, Oracle could use customer data collected by TikTok to improve its marketing products, but spending tens of billions to acquire a consumer social media company would be a significant departure for the company. Oracle has struggled to find new avenues of growth as Amazon Web Services has dominated cloud computing, followed by Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud. In Oracle’s fiscal fourth quarter,  revenue declined 6% to $10.4 billion. Oracle has a history of being acquisitive but has slowed down on large deals in recent years.
 TikTok started a website and  Twitter account to address misinformation in real time. “Let us set the record straight,” the company said on the website. “TikTok has never provided any U.S. user data to the Chinese government, nor would it do so if asked.” My basic feeling is, if you have to say “let us set the record straight,” you have already lost the argument. This is the 2020 version of the old Facebook “Hard Questions” blog, and seems likely to have about the same level of impact on brand perception. (Nathan Crooks / Bloomberg)
 Advertisers are rethinking plans to advertise on  TikTok ahead of a potential US ban. Privacy concerns have also prompted some to avoid the app. (Anissa Gardizy /  The Information)
 TikTok launched a creator ambassador program in 2019 to help a small group of influencers grow their followings on the app. The program has several perks, like all-expenses-paid trips, connections with brands, and access to new app features. Get those benefits while you can, hype houses! (Amanda Perelli /  Business Insider)
 TikTokers are posting videos making fun of their  Trump-supporting parents. So, at least one good thing has come out of quarantine. (Kadia Goba /  BuzzFeed)
 Snapchat is testing a feature that would allow users to share Snap originals, shows and publisher stories with friends off the platform. It’s an expansion of a previous push the company made to get users to share stories outside of its app. (Sara Fischer /  Axios)
 Oculus will soon require all of its virtual reality headset users to sign up with a  Facebook account. The Facebook-owned company says it will start removing support for separate Oculus accounts in October. (Adi Robertson /  The Verge)
 Facebook and NYU are working on a project that uses AI to make MRI scans four times faster. The scientists trained a machine learning model to “predict” what final MRI scans look like from just a quarter of the usual input data. (James Vincent /  The Verge)
 While some companies embrace remote work,  Amazon is doubling down on its office infrastructure. The e-commerce giant is expanding its physical offices in six US cities including New York and adding thousands of corporate jobs in those areas — and without the enormous subsidies it once demanded as part of its sham HQ2 search. (Sebastian Herrera /  The Wall Street Journal)

在成为第一家市值达到1万亿美元的美国公司仅仅两年后，苹果正式将其市值翻了一番，达到2万亿美元。这一非凡的成就意味着它与一家非科技公司并驾齐驱，因为它有权吹嘘自己是世界上最有价值的公司-目前是这样。
2万亿美元的市值是基于苹果股票突破每股467.77美元的价格，乘以该公司最近公布的4275,634,000股流通股。尽管如此，苹果近年来一直在积极回购自己的股票，仅在上个季度就减少了5870万股。因此，尽管苹果自己的股票应用程序显示出2万亿美元的估值，但实际市值可能与这个数字略有不同。
在苹果今天触及官方里程碑之前，股价在464美元的区间内交易，在短暂推高至467.97美元之前曾在467美元附近徘徊，然后又突破了468美元。由于今天的收盘价将是苹果当天最高和最低价格的平均值，因此该股需要触及约473.10美元，才能在交易时段收于467.77美元以上。
严格来说，苹果是第二家达到2万亿美元水平的公司，因为沙特阿拉伯石油公司(沙特阿美)在2019年12月首次达到这个数字，原因是当时对化石燃料的强劲需求。随后，沙特阿美受到与新冠肺炎相关的旅行减少和油价下跌的打击，以及对其资产价值的怀疑，其市值最近徘徊在1.8万亿美元(6.68万亿沙特里亚尔)附近。但在苹果距离其之前的纪录仅咫尺之遥之前不久，这家石油生产商又回到了2万亿美元大关。相比之下，苹果最接近的科技公司竞争对手-Alphabet、亚马逊和微软-都在苹果之后突破了1万亿美元大关，但今天它们的市值比库比蒂诺公司落后数千亿美元。
尽管全球疫情肆虐，但苹果在过去两个季度创造了非常强劲的收入，分析人士预计，在可预见的未来，该公司的好时光将继续下去。随着客户寻求在家工作和在家上学的解决方案，Mac和iPad的价格都出现了上涨，而苹果的流媒体、保修和支付处理服务继续快速增长。广受欢迎的iPhone和Apple Watch预计将在今年秋天进行更新，仅略高于9月份的正常时间表，不久之后，第一批基于ARM的新“Apple Silicon”Mac也将紧随其后。
苹果还宣布，将以4：1的比例拆分股票，在8月31日以四分之一的价格出售股票-此举将允许新的投资者买入，可能会推动新一轮稳定增长，而不管该公司在其最大市场之一中国的未来如何。尽管与中国政府合作，但由于特朗普政府的剑拔弩张，苹果面临着与贸易相关的限制威胁，这威胁到了苹果在该地区的硬件生产和销售。
note_en:
Just two years after becoming the  first U.S. company to reach a $1 trillion market capitalization, Apple has officially doubled its value to $2 trillion. The remarkable feat means it’s tied with a non-tech firm for bragging rights as the world’s most valuable company — for now.
 The $2 trillion market cap is based on Apple’s stock breaking a price of $467.77 per share, multiplied by the company’s most recent report of 4,275,634,000 outstanding shares. That said, Apple has aggressively repurchased its own shares in recent years and reduced the number by 58.7 million during the past quarter alone. As a result, though Apple’s own Stocks app showed a $2 trillion valuation, the actual market capitalization may vary slightly from that number.
  Prior to Apple hitting the official milestone today, shares were trading in the $464 range, flirting with $467 before briefly pushing to $467.97, and then above $468. As today’s closing share price will be the average of Apple’s highest and lowest prices for the day, the stock would need to hit roughly $473.10 to close above $467.77 for the trading session.
 Apple is technically the second company to reach the $2 trillion level, as the  Saudi Arabian Oil Company — also known as Saudi Aramco — first hit that number in December 2019 on the strength of then-solid demand for fossil fuels. Aramco was subsequently hit by COVID-19-related travel declines and falling oil prices, as well as skepticism over the value of its assets, and its market cap recently hovered near $1.8 trillion (6.68 trillion Saudi riyals). But the oil producer returned to the $2 trillion mark shortly before Apple came within striking distance of its prior record. By contrast, Apple’s closest tech company rivals — Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft — all crossed the $1 trillion mark after Apple, but today their market caps trail the Cupertino company’s by hundreds of billions of dollars.
 Despite the global pandemic, Apple has generated  remarkably strong revenues over the past two quarters, and analysts expect the company’s good times to continue for the foreseeable future. Macs and iPads have both seen upticks as customers sought work from home and school from home solutions, while Apple’s media streaming, warranty, and payment processing services have continued to grow at a brisk pace. Updates to the popular  iPhone and Apple Watch are expected this fall, only slightly off the normal September schedule, with the first new  ARM-based “Apple Silicon” Macs following soon thereafter.
 Apple has also announced it will  split its stock by a 4:1 ratio, making shares available on August 31 for a quarter of their prior price — a move that will allow new investors to buy in, likely fueling another round of steady growth, regardless of questions about the company’s future in China, one of its largest markets. Despite  cooperating with the  Chinese government, Apple has faced the threat of trade-related restrictions due to  the Trump administration’s saber rattling, which has imperiled both its  hardware production and  sales in the region.
title: 苹果成为盘中市值达到2T美元的首家美国公司，两年前市值达到1T美元
title_en: Apple becomes the first US company to reach a $2T market cap during intraday trading, two years after reaching $1T

自本月早些时候宣布后，Android TV的新“电影亮点”(又名主屏幕广告)现在正向Android电视用户广泛推出，推出赞助内容、“员工精选”(Staff Pickers)等。
通过Android TV Home和Android TV核心服务应用程序的最新更新，谷歌为每个人首次推出了这些新的主屏幕广告。一些用户在上周左右就收到了这些更新，但从我们所知，这些更新似乎在本周初就已经可以广泛使用了。
那么，这些主屏幕广告是由什么组成的呢？当你启动你的Android电视设备时，你会在主屏幕的顶部看到一排新的，在赞助的内容或“Google Play员工精选”之间循环。没有自动播放音频或视频，你的应用程序行仍然突出显示，但新的广告占据了你屏幕的很大一部分，如图所示。
在我的例子中，这一排显示了HBO Max的“日冕时代的爱情”(Love In The Time Of Corona)、迪斯尼+(Disney+)的“莫阿娜”(Moana)、孔雀(Peacock)的“智力”(Intelligence)和HBO Max的“美国泡菜”(American Pickle)的广告。在我家里，我也看到不同的Android电视设备上有不同的“推荐”。
就我个人而言，我并不是因为视觉设计有趣和相对干净就完全讨厌这些-至少这不是Fire TV或Roku的人-但我确实希望用户在显示的内容上有一些选择，也许可以过滤掉他们不想看到的内容。
几天来，我一直在Android TV Subreddit上跟踪用户关于这一变化的情况，显然，那里和Google Play评论的初步反应都不是积极的。没有人喜欢在未经同意的情况下在自己的主屏幕上投放广告，更糟糕的是，Android电视没有提供关闭这些功能的方法。嗯，至少在官方上是这样。
幸运的是，有一种方法可以抵挡Android电视上的“亮点”栏目，从而摆脱“员工精选”和其他主屏幕广告。要做到这一点，只需转到“设置”，进入“应用程序”，然后点击“查看所有应用程序”。然后，您需要显示系统应用程序并找到“Android TV Home”。找到该应用程序后，单击它并找到“卸载更新”。
当你回到你的主屏幕时，你会发现你的一些偏好已经被重置了，但是你也会发现广告不见了。之后，您需要进入Google Play商店的设置并禁用自动更新。否则，你最终只会在几小时或几天内重新打开广告。
这不是一个永久的方法，当然谷歌最终会强制更新版本，但在此期间，这应该会起到作用。
你正在阅读朝九晚五的谷歌新闻，这些专家日复一日地发布关于谷歌及其周边生态系统的新闻。请务必查看我们的主页以获取所有最新新闻，并关注Twitter、Facebook和LinkedIn上的朝九晚五谷歌，随时了解最新消息。不知道从何说起？查看我们的独家故事、评论、操作指南，并订阅我们的YouTube频道
note_en:
After  being announced earlier this month, Android TV’s new “cinematic highlights” — aka homescreen ads — are now rolling out widely to Android TV users, pushing sponsored content, “Staff Picks,” and more.
  Through the latest updates to the  Android TV Home and  Android TV Core Services applications, Google has debuted these new homescreen ads for everyone. Some users got these updates within the last week or so, but from what we can tell the updates seem to be widely available as of the start of this week.
 So, what do these homescreen ads consist of? When you launch your Android TV device, you’ll see a new row at the top of the homescreen which cycles through content that is sponsored or a “Google Play Staff Pick.” There’s no auto-playing audio or video and your app row is still highlighted, but the new ads take up a huge portion of your screen as pictured.
 In my case, the row is showing an ad for “Love in the time of Corona” from HBO Max, “Moana” on Disney+, “Intelligence” on Peacock, and “American Pickle” on HBO Max. I’m also seeing different “recommendations” on different Android TV devices in my home.
 Personally, I don’t totally hate these just because the visual design is interesting and relatively clean — at least this isn’t Fire TV or Roku folks — but I do wish the user had  some choice in what’s shown, perhaps filtering out content they don’t want to see.
   I’ve been tracking users on the  Android TV subreddit regarding this change for a few days now and, obviously, initial reactions there and in  Google Play reviews have not been positive. No one is a fan of ads being dumped on their homescreen without consent, and worse yet Android TV doesn’t offer a way to turn these off. Well, at least officially.
 Luckily, there is a way to fend off the “highlights” row on Android TV to get rid of “Staff Picks” and other homescreen ads. To do so, simply go to  Settings, head to  Apps, and tap “ See All Apps.” You’ll then need to  show system apps and locate “ Android TV Home.” Once you find that application, click on it and locate “ Uninstall Updates.”
 When you head back to your homescreen, you’ll find that some of your preferences have been reset, but you’ll also find that the ads are gone. After that, you’ll need to go into the Google Play Store’s settings and disable automatic updates. Otherwise, you’ll just end up with the ads being turned back on within a few hours or days.
 This is  not a permanent method and surely Google will eventually force the updated version, but in the meantime, this should do the trick.
  You’re reading 9to5Google — experts who break news about Google and its surrounding ecosystem, day after day. Be sure to check out  our homepage for all the latest news, and follow 9to5Google on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start? Check out our  exclusive stories,  reviews,  how-tos, and  subscribe to our YouTube channel

Apple hit a market cap of $2 trillion, doubling in valuation in just over two years.
  Apple was also the first publicly traded U.S. company to reach a $1 trillion market cap.
 Apple hit a market cap of $2 trillion Wednesday, doubling in valuation in just over two years. It's the first publicly traded U.S. company to reach the $2 trillion milestone.
 Apple's share price hit $467.77, which pushed it over a $2 trillion market cap just before 11 a.m. ET. It hit the $2 trillion mark with a share price of $467.77 based on an outstanding share count of 4,275,634,000 shares.
  Apple  first reached a $1 trillion market cap on Aug. 2, 2018. Wall Street has largely expected Apple to be the first to surpass the $2 trillion mark. On July 31, Apple surpassed the state oil giant  Saudi Aramco to become the  world's most valuable publicly traded company.
 It didn't take long for other tech giants to match Apple's $1 trillion market cap. Today,  Amazon,  Microsoft and  Alphabet all have market caps above $1 trillion.
 Apple shares are up 59.24% year-to-date, pushing through the pandemic that's battered many companies. In the past year, shares have risen 122.3%.
 The company posted a historically strong third quarter late July, including $59.7 billion in revenue and double-digit growth in its products and services segments. Apple saw widespread retail closures during the quarter, especially in the United States, but touted both work-from-home trends and strong online sales as delivering a boost to overall operations.
